briefs.title: Disaster Financial Assistance Applications Now Require Upfront Documentation and Follow a New Approval Process
briefs.summary: BC updated its disaster financial assistance rules so businesses must now submit a full application — including invoices and supporting documents — right away, instead of just giving notice. The Provincial Emergency Program will now review eligibility first, then send approved claims to an adjuster, and make the final call on how much assistance is paid.
briefs.action_required: If you apply for disaster financial assistance, submit your full application and all invoices immediately — not just a notice.

key_dates.label: BC Compensation and Disaster Financial Assistance Regulation amendments take effect
